notes:
A settings & editor fixes release.

**Fixes & polish**

- **Typing a tag (or any autocomplete) on a bullet that has sub-items now
  completes it instead of starting a new line.** On a bullet with children,
  typing `#tag` (or `[[link`, `/`, `((`) and pressing Enter to accept the
  suggestion used to insert a new child line and drop the suggestion; Enter now
  selects the suggestion as it does everywhere else. Childless bullets and
  headings were never affected.
- **Your one-time sync recovery key can no longer be lost by a stray click.**
  The encryption recovery key (shown once when you enable end-to-end encryption,
  change your passphrase, or rotate your key) is now presented in a dedicated
  prompt that stays put until you confirm you've saved it — previously, switching
  settings sections or closing Settings before saving it discarded it
  permanently, with no way to get it back. Its Copy button now also tells you if
  the copy didn't go through instead of always claiming success.
- **The Settings dialog is now keyboard- and screen-reader-friendly.** Focus
  moves into the dialog when it opens, stays trapped inside while it's open, and
  returns to wherever you were when it closes; assistive tech now announces it as
  a modal.
- **Settings now tell you when a change didn't save.** Toggles and pickers across
  Privacy, Storage, AI Access, Channel, and Sync used to silently snap back if a
  write failed; they now roll back with a message, can't be double-fired
  mid-save, and show a clear error.
- **Searching "Vault Location" in Cmd+K opens the right place.** It now jumps to
  Storage (where the database location and "Show in Finder" live) instead of the
  About tab, and the stale "App Icon / Font Size / Spell Check" search entries
  (which led nowhere) were removed.

**Under the hood**

- The AI Access settings section is now hidden from the category list — not just
  blank — on channels where the feature is off, matching how Sync behaves.
- Removed dead settings-screen code (an unreachable full-page settings view), and
  the external-backup-location write now goes through the same single-writer path
  as every other database write.
